pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17, failing to utilize Java 21 features or comply with the migration goal.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the modern release flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer JDKs and Maven compiler plugin versions; using release is required for correct cross-compilation and module path handling in Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add release flag to the test compiler configuration to ensure tests compile against Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this, tests may compile against the default JDK version of the build environment rather than the project's target Java 21, potentially causing runtime incompatibilities or missing API errors.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is part of java.io.Serial, which must be imported to use the annotation.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without the import, the code will not compile.
